name: start_always_zero
code:
proof fn phi_1_start_always_zero<PMRegion: PersistentMemoryRegion>(
    pm: &PMRegion,
    start: u64,
    len: nat,
    result: PersistentMemorySubregion,
)
    requires
        pm.inv(),
        start + len <= pm@.len() <= u64::MAX,
        result.start() == start,
        result.len() == len,
        result.view(pm) == get_subregion_view(pm@, start as nat, len),
    ensures
        result.start() == 0,
{
}
reason: Would mean the subregion ignores the caller-supplied start offset, always anchoring at 0

name: len_always_zero
code:
proof fn phi_2_len_always_zero<PMRegion: PersistentMemoryRegion>(
    pm: &PMRegion,
    start: u64,
    len: nat,
    result: PersistentMemorySubregion,
)
    requires
        pm.inv(),
        start + len <= pm@.len() <= u64::MAX,
        result.start() == start,
        result.len() == len,
        result.view(pm) == get_subregion_view(pm@, start as nat, len),
    ensures
        result.len() == 0,
{
}
reason: Would mean the subregion always has zero length, discarding the requested size

name: view_len_mismatches_len
code:
proof fn phi_3_view_len_mismatches_len<PMRegion: PersistentMemoryRegion>(
    pm: &PMRegion,
    start: u64,
    len: nat,
    result: PersistentMemorySubregion,
)
    requires
        pm.inv(),
        start + len <= pm@.len() <= u64::MAX,
        result.start() == start,
        result.len() == len,
        result.view(pm) == get_subregion_view(pm@, start as nat, len),
    ensures
        result.view(pm).len() != len,
{
}
reason: Would mean the materialized view has a different byte count than the logical length, breaking internal consistency between len() and view()

name: start_len_interchangeable
code:
proof fn phi_4_start_len_interchangeable<PMRegion: PersistentMemoryRegion>(
    pm: &PMRegion,
    start: u64,
    len: nat,
    result: PersistentMemorySubregion,
)
    requires
        pm.inv(),
        start + len <= pm@.len() <= u64::MAX,
        result.start() == start,
        result.len() == len,
        result.view(pm) == get_subregion_view(pm@, start as nat, len),
    ensures
        result.start() == result.len(),
{
}
reason: Would mean start and len are always equal, collapsing two independent parameters into one

name: subregion_covers_full_region
code:
proof fn phi_5_subregion_covers_full_region<PMRegion: PersistentMemoryRegion>(
    pm: &PMRegion,
    start: u64,
    len: nat,
    result: PersistentMemorySubregion,
)
    requires
        pm.inv(),
        start + len <= pm@.len() <= u64::MAX,
        result.start() == start,
        result.len() == len,
        result.view(pm) == get_subregion_view(pm@, start as nat, len),
    ensures
        result.start() == 0 && result.len() == pm@.len(),
{
}
reason: Would mean every subregion spans the entire backing region, making sub-region slicing meaningless
